html:
Exclusive to:  

The Dripple Pipes is a craftable musical instrument introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. It can be found in the Magic Tab. It requires a Piratihatitator to prototype, and can be crafted using 1 Horn, 2 Nightmare Fuel, and 1 Rope. It has 5 uses. When used, it creates a light Rain for a half-day (4 minutes), unless it is Winter, in which it will create a light Snow instead.

The Dripple Pipes will not function if used in Caves or Ruins.

Contents

  • 1 Hamlet
  • 2 Tips
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Gallery

Hamlet

In the Hamlet DLC, the Dripple Pipes crafting recipe is unavailable, however, a Blueprint for it can be bought with 100 Oincs from The Tinkerer's Tower. The Horn is available at 'The Sty' Oddities Emporium. When used in Humid Season, it creates Heavy Fog instead.

Tips

  • Rain will allow Mushrooms to grow.
  • In rain, Farm crops grow up to 300% faster.
  • Rain will cause Flowers to spawn.
  • Bringing it to a Hamlet world through the Skyworthy is a good way to make it rain to allow Nettle Vines to grow.
  • Bringing it to a Reign of Giants world through the Seaworthy is a good way to prevent wildfires during Summer.
  • Dripple Pipes work well with the Morning Star, as they make enemies wet.

Trivia

  • Dripple Pipes were introduced in the Release the Quacken update.
  • It shares an animation with the pan flute.

Gallery

  • Walani standing next to dropped Dripple Pipes.
  • Walani playing the Dripple Pipes.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

–Wigfrid
“It's just begging to be played!”
–Webber
“I'm gonna play some sweet jams on this baby.”
–Walani
“It plays a lilting tune.”
–Warly
“A flute ta play a lil diddy.”
–Woodlegs
“WILBA PLAY THE FOOD O' LOVE”
–Wilba
“Toot! Toot!”
–Wormwood
“If I play it out the other end does it make it not rain?”
–Wheeler
The Dripple Pipes is a craftable musical instrument introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. It can be found in the Magic Tab. It requires a Piratihatitator to prototype, and can be crafted using 1 Horn, 2 Nightmare Fuel, and 1 Rope. It has 5 uses. When used, it creates a light Rain for a half-day (4 minutes), unless it is Winter, in which it will create a light Snow instead.
The Dripple Pipes will not function if used in Caves or Ruins.

In the Hamlet DLC, the Dripple Pipes crafting recipe is unavailable, however, a Blueprint for it can be bought with 100 Oincs from The Tinkerer's Tower. The Horn is available at 'The Sty' Oddities Emporium. When used in Humid Season, it creates Heavy Fog instead.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Rain will allow Mushrooms to grow.
In rain, Farm crops grow up to 300% faster.
Rain will cause Flowers to spawn.
Bringing it to a Hamlet world through the Skyworthy is a good way to make it rain to allow Nettle Vines to grow.
Bringing it to a Reign of Giants world through the Seaworthy is a good way to prevent wildfires during Summer.
Dripple Pipes work well with the Morning Star, as they make enemies wet.

Dripple Pipes were introduced in the Release the Quacken update.
It shares an animation with the pan flute.

The '''Dripple Pipes''' is a craftable musical instrument introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. It can be found in the [[Magic Tab]]. It requires a [[Piratihatitator]] to prototype, and can be crafted using 1 [[Horn]], 2 [[Nightmare Fuel]], and 1 [[Rope]]. It has 5 uses. When used, it creates a light [[Rain]] for a half-day (4 minutes), unless it is [[Winter]], in which it will create a light [[Snow]] instead.

The Dripple Pipes will not function if used in [[Caves]] or [[Ruins]].

== {{Pic32|Hamlet icon}} Hamlet ==
In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, the Dripple Pipes crafting recipe is unavailable, however, a [[Blueprint]] for it can be bought with 100 [[Oinc]]s from [[The Tinkerer's Tower]]. The Horn is available at [['The Sty' Oddities Emporium]]. When used in [[Seasons/Humid|Humid]] Season, it creates [[Heavy Fog]] instead.

=={{pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Rain will allow [[Mushroom]]s to grow.
* In rain, [[Farm]] crops grow up to 300% faster.
* Rain will cause [[Flower]]s to spawn.
* Bringing it to a ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' world through the [[Skyworthy]] is a good way to make it rain to allow [[Nettle Vine]]s to grow.
* Bringing it to a [[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|''Reign of Giants'']] world through the [[Seaworthy]] is a good way to prevent wildfires during [[Summer]].
*Dripple Pipes work well with the [[Morning Star]], as they make enemies wet.

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Dripple Pipes were introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked#February 25, 2016 - Release the Quacken Update|Release the Quacken]]'' update.
* It shares an animation with the pan flute.
